prior.content_md:
**Who must register**
Registration under the UK SAF Mandate is mandatory for any company that owns and supplies 15.9 terajoules (approximately 468,000 litres) or more of relevant aviation turbine fuel for use in the UK during an obligation year (1 January to 31 December). Suppliers already registered with the DfT Administrator under the Renewable Transport Fuel Obligation (RTFO) do not need to re-register but must notify the Administrator that they are now also subject to the SAF Mandate. The registration process requires beginning account-opening within 28 days of first becoming an obligated supplier.

**Voluntary registration**
Companies supplying below the 15.9 TJ threshold may voluntarily register if they wish to claim SAF certificates. Any company supplying low-carbon avtur, avgas, or hydrogen for use in aviation in the UK may claim SAF Mandate certificates and trade them on the open market.

**Registration data fields required**
Registration requires: the company's legal identity (name, legal status — e.g., PLC, limited company, partnership, sole trader); individual proof of identity for at least one director who will be responsible for ensuring organisational compliance; and relevant fuel supply volume data. The Administrator operates a Reporting Operating System (ROS) through which account holders submit certificate applications and redemptions electronically.

**Reporting scope and cycle**
Obligated suppliers must submit, via the online ROS platform, annual reports covering: (a) quantities of relevant aviation turbine fuel supplied in the UK during the obligation period; (b) quantities and specification of any SAF supplied; (c) evidence that SAF was supplied for use in UK aviation (e.g., bills of lading, delivery records); and (d) carbon and sustainability data for claimed SAF. HMRC fuel quantity data cross-verifies supplier-reported volumes. Provisional data may be submitted up to 7 months after the obligation period ends; final figures are typically published in the winter following the reporting year.

**Status of published guidance**
Full compliance guidance (SAF Mandate Compliance Guidance 2025), technical guidance (RTFO and SAF Mandate Technical Information 2025), and third-party assurance guidance (RTFO and SAF Mandate Third Party Assurance) have all been published by DfT. Updated 2026 versions of these documents have also been released. A call for evidence on crop-derived SAF was opened by DfT in December 2025, which may result in changes to eligible feedstock categories.

*Source: Register an Organisation under the RTFO or SAF Mandate, DfT, Dec 2024. https://www.gov.uk/guidance/register-an-organisation-under-the-rtfo-or-saf-mandate; SAF Mandate Compliance Guidance 2025, DfT, Jan 2025. https://assets.publishing.service.gov.uk/media/6762717b26a2d1ff182534f7/saf-mandate-compliance-guidance-2025.pdf; SAF Mandate Statistics, DfT, Feb 2026. https://www.gov.uk/government/collections/sustainable-aviation-fuel-saf-mandate-statistics*
